Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are going to create an event log with the following columns Case ID order_id Activity Name Timestamp Resource Actor optional but we will include if available Other Attributes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Cases We use order_id as the case identifier

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Orders Activity Order Created Timestamp order_date Resource sales_person_id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Lines Activity Items Picked Timestamp item_picking_date Resource picker_id

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since each line is picked by a different picker or at a different time and we want to capture the resource and the time it is meaningful to have one event per line

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Delivery We have two events Order Dispatched at the delivery dispatch date and Order Arrived at the delivery arrival date

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: We will use the carrier id for both events because the carrier is the one delivering and the table does not have a dispatcher

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Invoice Activity Invoice Issued Timestamp invoice issue date Resource billing person id

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Payments Activity Payment Received Timestamp payment date Resource leave blank because the table does not have that information

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For payments we need to add the order id by joining the payment table with the invoice table to get the order id

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can include other attributes such as customer id order priority item id quantity delivery note delivery id invoice amount invoice id payment method payment reference

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For delivery we break each delivery record into two events one for dispatch and one for arrival and include the same delivery note in both events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 1 Create events from Orders table Step 2 Create events from Order Lines table Step 3 Create events from Delivery table two events per row Step 4 Create events from Invoice table Step 5 Create events from Payments table joined with Invoice on invoice id

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1001 has Order Created two Items Picked one delivery producing two events one invoice and one payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1002 has Order Created two Items Picked two deliveries producing four events two invoices and two payments

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1003 has Order Created two Items Picked one delivery producing two events one invoice and one payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1004 has Order Created and one Items Picked event and no delivery no invoice no payment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Order 1005 has Order Created two Items Picked one delivery producing two events one invoice and one payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We have to be cautious the timestamps must be in chronological order We are generating events and then we will sort by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We combine all these events and sort by Case ID and Timestamp

Name: Validation - IND
Text: We must include every event for every order so we must have at least one event for every order id

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Total events calculations were reconsidered several times including recounting orders order lines deliveries invoices and payments and rechecking case by case totals

Name: Validation - NE
Text: The reasoning includes inconsistent event counts such as 31 33 and 34 total events and confusion about the number of order line rows

Name: Backtracking - NE
Text: I see the mistake I mistakenly said 8 for delivery earlier No the delivery table has 5 records so 10 events

Name: Validation - NE
Text: I think there is a typo in the problem Given the time I will proceed with the 8 order lines events
